100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 11, 1926, the British General Strike entered its second week, with workers across various industries standing firm against wage cuts and poor working conditions, leading to widespread disruptions. In Germany, tensions escalated as the government faced mounting pressure from both the left and right, threatening the stability of the Weimar Republic. Meanwhile, the United States continued to grapple with the aftermath of Prohibition, as bootlegging operations flourished and law enforcement struggled to maintain order.
Updated: Monday, May 11, 2026 at 09:13:59 AM CDT
